items.title: Tesla expands ‘Robotaxi’ to entire Austin metro — but still has only ~20 vehicles
items.topic: Tesla expands ‘Robotaxi’ to entire Austin metro — but still has only ~20 vehicles
items.summary: Tesla has expanded its Robotaxi service to the entire Austin metro area, but the fleet still consists of only about 20 vehicles. This limited deployment highlights the ongoing challenges in scaling autonomous vehicle services, despite Tesla's ambitious plans.
items.abstract: Tesla has expanded its Robotaxi service to the entire Austin metro area, but the fleet still consists of only about 20 vehicles. This limited deployment highlights the ongoing challenges in scaling autonomous vehicle services, despite Tesla's ambitious plans.

items.title: Tesla’s own AI trainers don’t trust ‘Full Self-Driving’ or its safety stats, Reuters finds
items.topic: Tesla’s own AI trainers don’t trust ‘Full Self-Driving’ or its safety stats, Reuters finds
items.summary: Tesla's internal AI trainers have expressed significant doubts about the safety and reliability of the company's Full Self-Driving (FSD) technology. According to Reuters, these concerns include issues with the system's perception and decision-making capabilities, raising questions about Tesla's claims regarding FSD's safety.
items.abstract: Tesla's internal AI trainers have expressed significant doubts about the safety and reliability of the company's Full Self-Driving (FSD) technology. According to Reuters, these concerns include issues with the system's perception and decision-making capabilities, raising questions about Tesla's claims regarding FSD's safety.

items.title: Why Tesla’s AI trainers don’t trust its self-driving tech – or its safety stats
items.topic: Why Tesla’s AI trainers don’t trust its self-driving tech – or its safety stats
items.summary: Tesla's internal AI trainers have raised serious concerns about the safety and reliability of the company's Full Self-Driving (FSD) technology. These doubts, reported by Reuters, focus on the system's perception and decision-making, challenging Tesla's public safety claims.
items.abstract: Tesla's internal AI trainers have raised serious concerns about the safety and reliability of the company's Full Self-Driving (FSD) technology. These doubts, reported by Reuters, focus on the system's perception and decision-making, challenging Tesla's public safety claims.

items.title: Tesla patents camera wiper for self-driving — resulting in more doubts for FSD owners
items.topic: Tesla patents camera wiper for self-driving — resulting in more doubts for FSD owners
items.summary: Tesla has patented a camera wiper system for its self-driving vehicles, which has led to increased skepticism among FSD owners. The patent suggests that Tesla is addressing hardware issues that could affect the performance of its autonomous driving technology.
items.abstract: Tesla has patented a camera wiper system for its self-driving vehicles, which has led to increased skepticism among FSD owners. The patent suggests that Tesla is addressing hardware issues that could affect the performance of its autonomous driving technology.

items.title: NVIDIA Launches Cosmos 3, the Open Frontier Foundation Model for Physical AI
items.topic: NVIDIA Launches Cosmos 3, the Open Frontier Foundation Model for Physical AI
items.summary: NVIDIA has launched Cosmos 3, an open-source foundation model for physical AI. This model is designed to advance research in areas such as robotics and autonomous vehicles, providing a powerful tool for developers and researchers in the field.
items.abstract: NVIDIA has launched Cosmos 3, an open-source foundation model for physical AI. This model is designed to advance research in areas such as robotics and autonomous vehicles, providing a powerful tool for developers and researchers in the field.

items.title: Illinois Lawmakers Just Passed America’s Strongest AI Safety Bill
items.topic: Illinois Lawmakers Just Passed America’s Strongest AI Safety Bill
items.summary: Illinois lawmakers have passed a bill that is considered the strongest AI safety legislation in the United States. The bill includes provisions for transparency, accountability, and ethical use of AI, with direct implications for the development and deployment of autonomous vehicles.
items.abstract: Illinois lawmakers have passed a bill that is considered the strongest AI safety legislation in the United States. The bill includes provisions for transparency, accountability, and ethical use of AI, with direct implications for the development and deployment of autonomous vehicles.

items.title: Run Step 3.7 Flash on NVIDIA GPUs with Enterprise-Ready Multimodal AI | NVIDIA Technical Blog
items.topic: Run Step 3.7 Flash on NVIDIA GPUs with Enterprise-Ready Multimodal AI | NVIDIA Technical Blog
items.summary: Uber has committed nearly $500 million to Nuro, a self-driving startup, according to sources. This significant investment underscores Uber's continued interest in autonomous delivery and transportation technologies.

items.title: Inside Tesla's self-driving machine
items.summary: Reuters provides an in-depth look at Tesla's self-driving machine, highlighting the company's approach to developing and deploying autonomous driving technology. The article delves into the technical and operational aspects of Tesla's FSD system.

items.title: OpenAI's Altman to urge US lawmakers not to require AI model approvals
items.topic: OpenAI's Altman to urge US lawmakers not to require AI model approvals
items.summary: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman is set to urge U.S. lawmakers not to require mandatory approvals for AI models. This stance reflects the tech industry's push for lighter regulation, which could impact the development and deployment of AI in autonomous vehicles.
items.abstract: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman is set to urge U.S. lawmakers not to require mandatory approvals for AI models. This stance reflects the tech industry's push for lighter regulation, which could impact the development and deployment of AI in autonomous vehicles.

items.title: Exclusive: Uber's commitment to self-driving startup Nuro is close to $500 mln, sources say
items.topic: Exclusive: Uber's commitment to self-driving startup Nuro is close to $500 mln, sources say
items.summary: Uber is close to a $500 million investment in Nuro, a self-driving startup, according to sources. This significant commitment indicates Uber's strategic focus on autonomous delivery and transportation technologies, which could have long-term implications for the AV industry.
